title: urtica thunbergiana subsp. dentata
intro: Urtica thunbergiana subsp. dentata, commonly known as the Japanese nettle or toothed nettle, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the nettle family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The medicinal properties of Urtica thunbergiana subsp. dentata include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve kidney function, providing health benefits such as relieving arthritis symptoms and improving urinary tract health. Urtica thunbergiana subsp. dentata can be used to make herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and infusion, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
